meaning: adj.(美国第二十八任总统)威尔逊的
examples: The Wilsonian spirit—the belief that the whole is greater than the sum of its parts.
But the old Wilsonian notion of a governing world body has been laid to rest.
Roosevelt and Harry Truman attempted to craft a new "community of power" based upon Wilsonian principles.
It's a system designed to protect the rights of states to Wilsonian "self determination, " not to subject them to the will of the strongest.
